paper_metadata.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
paper_metadata.abstract: While the Transformer architecture has become the de-facto standard for natural language processing tasks, its applications to computer vision remain limited. In vision, attention is either applied in conjunction with convolutional networks, or used to replace certain components of convolutional networks while keeping their overall structure in place. We show that this reliance on CNNs is not necessary and a pure transformer applied directly to sequences of image patches can perform very well on image classification tasks. When pre-trained on large amounts of data and transferred to multiple mid-sized or small image recognition benchmarks (ImageNet, CIFAR-100, VTAB, etc.), Vision Transformer (ViT) attains excellent results compared to state-of-the-art convolutional networks while requiring substantially fewer computational resources to train.

extraction_data.innovation: Vision Transformer (ViT) applies a standard Transformer encoder directly to sequences of image patches, treating them as tokens. This approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on image classification tasks when pre-trained on large-scale datasets, demonstrating that pure Transformers can replace convolutional networks in vision without relying on inductive biases like locality or translation equivariance.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning on mid-sized and small image recognition benchmarks (ImageNet, CIFAR-100, VTAB)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on image recognition benchmarks
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Introduces the Vision Transformer (ViT) architecture, which treats image patches as tokens and applies a pure Transformer encoder to perform image classification, demonstrating that Transformers can achieve state-of-the-art performance on image recognition tasks when pre-trained on large-scale datasets.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning, computer vision
extraction_data.extension: Uses a patch-based tokenization scheme where each 16×16 patch of an image is linearly embedded and treated as a token, enabling the use of standard Transformer architectures for vision tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Vision Transformer (ViT) introduces a pure Transformer encoder architecture for image recognition by treating images as sequences of patches, replacing convolutional inductive biases with learnable position embeddings and self-attention mechanisms.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning, fine-tuning on diverse vision benchmarks
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Image recognition via self-supervised or supervised pre-training (implied by context)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning on downstream classification tasks
extraction_data.extension: Uses relative positional embeddings to improve performance in attention mechanisms, enabling better modeling of spatial relationships between image patches.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Demonstrates that Vision Transformers pre-trained on smaller public datasets like ImageNet-21k can still achieve strong performance on downstream tasks, especially when combined with proper regularization and fine-tuning, showing that large-scale pre-training is not strictly necessary for good transfer performance.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning, computer vision
extraction_data.extension: Uses a patch-based tokenization scheme where each 14×14 patch of an image is linearly embedded and treated as a token, enabling the use of standard Transformer architectures for vision tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale

extraction_data.innovation: Demonstrates that Vision Transformers pre-trained on smaller public datasets like ImageNet-21k can still achieve strong performance on downstream tasks, especially when combined with proper regularization and fine-tuning, showing that large-scale pre-training is not strictly necessary for good transfer performance.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, transfer learning, computer vision
extraction_data.extension: Uses a patch-based tokenization scheme where each 16×16 patch of an image is linearly embedded and treated as a token, enabling the use of standard Transformer architectures for vision tasks.
extraction_data.paper_title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
